content: Just as the linguistic concept of reflexion (reflection) in high school English education asks learners to map their own thinking process back onto written text to refine expression, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow this pedagogical framework to require models to 'rewrite their own draft responses' from the inconsistent self-contrast perspectives documented in existing LLM reflection research. This turns abstract technical self-correction into a structured, learnable process that mirrors how human students improve through reflective writing exercises.
